violations.detail: All three cards are mirror-deducible from each other. Card 1 ('What is the primary function of mitochondria?' → 'Producing ATP') and Card 2 ('Where is ATP produced?' → 'Mitochondria') are a classic mirror pair — knowing one trivially gives you the other. Card 3 ('What molecule do mitochondria produce?' → 'ATP') is the same fact again in yet another framing. These should be collapsed to one card or reframed to test genuinely different knowledge.

All three cards test the same underlying fact: mitochondria produce ATP. Every card is a rephrasing of the same piece of knowledge. The rule states 'Every card must test a DIFFERENT piece of knowledge from the source content.'

All 3 cards are definitional/label-style ('what is', 'where is', 'what molecule'). 0% are why/how/reasoning cards. The target is ~40% conceptual understanding and why/how cards. No causal, application, or misconception cards are present. E.g., missing: 'Why do mitochondria have their own DNA?' or 'How does the electron transport chain generate ATP?'

violations.rule: Make cards FROM the provided content only
violations.detail: There is no source content visible — the agent appears to have generated cards from general knowledge about mitochondria rather than from specific content the user taught or pasted. The skill requires cards to be based on what was actually taught or provided.

Card 2 asks for a label/location ('Where is ATP produced?' → 'Mitochondria') without testing what mitochondria DO or HOW they produce ATP. This is a naming/location card, not a functional understanding card.

While there are technically 3 cards (meeting minimum cluster size), they all test the same fact. Effectively this is a 1-card cluster dressed up as 3, which fails the spirit of the minimum cluster rule requiring cards that 'cover different aspects of the topic.'

score_justification: The set has the correct table format and uses proper card types, but fundamentally fails on content: all three cards encode the same single fact (mitochondria produce ATP) rephrased three ways, violating mirror-deducibility, no-repeated-facts, and type distribution rules. No source content was referenced.
